siteFooter.brandTagline: Workforce infrastructure for any American city.
siteFooter.shippedLine: Built in Texas. Shipped to anywhere.

banner.stallBody: We've noticed you've been stuck for a while. A navigator can help you find the next step.

hero.title: What's standing between you and a job?
hero.subtitle: GoWork is a workforce navigator that helps you overcome barriers and find your path to employment.

home.heroQuestion: What's standing between you and a job?
home.heroBlurb: GoWork is a workforce navigator that helps you overcome barriers and find your path to employment.
home.stepAssessDesc: Answer a few questions about your situation, barriers, and work history
home.stepMatchDesc: We match you with jobs, resources, and transit routes in your area
home.stepPlanDesc: Get a personalized action plan: what to do Monday morning
home.bottomCtaBody: It takes about 2 minutes. No account needed.

ch1.eyebrow: HackFW 2026 · Fort Worth, TX · live in production
ch1.line2: There is a {{wall}} between you and {{job}}.
ch1.line3: We tear it {{down}} — brick by brick.
ch1.ariaLabel: There is a wall between you and a job. We tear it down, brick by brick.
ch1.subhead: {{seven}} stand between most workers and a paycheck. {{system}}
ch1.subheadSystem: GoWork sees all seven at once.

idle.orbitAria: The map is alive — markers are drifting

titleSequence.title: The Wall
titleSequence.subtitle: An interactive map of Fort Worth, Texas

ch2.stat1Cap: Texans hit a barrier this year that costs them a job they could have held.
ch2.stat2Cap: Average commute Carlos faces between his shift and his daughter's 2 p.m. dismissal — until we sequence the day.
ch2.stat3Cap: Distinct barriers in a single working week. None of them his work ethic. All of them connected.
ch2.stat4Cap: Wage of the fair-chance job 4.2 miles away — the destination the whole map orbits around.

ch3.portraitAlt: Stylized portrait of Carlos, a Fort Worth welder
ch3.captionQuote: I work nights at a warehouse. School pickup at 2. The bus runs every 47 minutes. You do the math.
ch3.p1: Carlos lives in {{zip}} — a Fort Worth ZIP where one in three adults has an open record from a warrant they didn't know about, a license they couldn't afford to clear, or a court date they couldn't take off work to attend.
ch3.p2: He has work history. He has references. He has a daughter who needs to be picked up from Como Elementary at 2:00. {{bold}} — until now.
ch3.p2Bold: What he doesn't have is a system that can hold all of those things in its head at once
ch3.ariaLabel: Meet Carlos — a father, a welder, not a case number

ch4.ariaLabel: The map — Tuesday in Fort Worth, four stops, one cleared day
ch4.fallbackTitle: Tuesday · Fort Worth · one cleared day
ch4.fallbackBody: DPS by ten. Workforce Solutions by twelve-thirty. Alcon shift at three-twenty-seven. The whole day, sequenced.

cards.card1Body: The day starts with three impossible appointments. DPS by 10. Court by 4. Pickup at 2. We rebuild the order so they fit.
cards.card2Body: One brick falls. Four jobs unblock. The license CDL employers need was 90 minutes away the whole time.
cards.card3Body: Three fair-chance matches. Already filtered. The case worker hands him a printed plan, not a stack of brochures.
cards.card4Body: The destination. $22.50 an hour, with insurance. Pre-apprenticeship pays through training. The day ends with him on the floor.

ch4.legendAria: Map legend — six layers, one cleared day

step.dropcap: Carlos clocks out at 3:27. Pickup at Como is at 2:00. DPS opens until 4:30. Bus 4 runs every 47 minutes. The math doesn't work — until you sequence around it. We rebuild his Tuesday around three hard constraints: the bus headway, the school bell, and a court date he can't move. The path that emerged isn't faster. It's survivable.
step.pull: Every barrier connects. We find the order.
step.tlEvent1: Bus 4 → downtown. Hemphill & Berry stop. $2 day pass — keep receipt.
step.tlEvent2: DPS · replace driver license. Slot held. Bring birth cert + SS card + lease.
step.tlEvent3: Workforce Solutions · check-in with Tanya R. She has your barrier list.
step.tlEvent4: Como Elementary · pick up Lily. Backup driver: auntie Rosa.
step.tlEvent5: Alcon · shift start. Pre-apprenticeship paid. The day ends with him on the floor.

ch5.ariaLabel: The plan — one plan, four moves, sequenced for your week
ch5.intro: Most plans hand you a list. We hand you an order — Monday clears the barriers, Tuesday sequences the day, Wednesday matches fair-chance jobs, Thursday runs the wage math. By Friday you have a written plan, in your language, that a case manager can sign on Day 1.
ch5.card1Body: Seven barriers, ranked by which ones unlock the most jobs first. License before court. Court before childcare. Order matters.
ch5.card2Body: DPS at 10, Workforce Solutions at 12:30, Alcon shift at 3:27. We hand you a route, a transit time, and a fallback for every stop.
ch5.card3Body: Three employers within six miles that hire through navigators, review records individually, and pay through training.
ch5.card4Body: $22.50 nets more than $18.50 in Tarrant County — but only after the cliff math. We show the real take-home before you sign.

card1.back1: Pull DPS records, court records, and any open warrants — in that order.
card1.back2: Map each barrier to the job it unlocks (license = CDL, court = bondable employers).
card1.back3: Flag the two we can clear this week. Park the rest.

card2.back1: Stack errands by transit corridor: DPS → Workforce Solutions → site visit.
card2.back2: Build a fallback for the bus that runs every 35 minutes.
card2.back3: Print the day. Tape it to the fridge. Show it to the case manager.

card3.back1: Three fair-chance employers within six miles, hiring this week.
card3.back2: Each one matched to your record type and shift availability.
card3.back3: All three pay through training — no unpaid pre-apprenticeship.

card4.back1: We compute SNAP, childcare, and Medicaid changes at the offered wage.
card4.back2: We show the real take-home — after the cliff — before you sign.
card4.back3: If a $2 raise costs $400, we tell you. Honest. On paper.

ch6.ariaLabel: Live jobs — three fair-chance employers within six miles

cards.alconBlurb: Hires through navigator referral. Background reviewed individually — no auto-disqualify. Pre-apprenticeship pays through training.
cards.bnsfBlurb: License unblocks this in 90 minutes at DPS. Trainee program pays from day one. Background reviewed by panel — felonies older than five years frequently approved.
cards.dunnBlurb: Pre-apprenticeship is paid. Tools provided. Scales to $38/hr at journeyman. JE Dunn partners with Tarrant fair-chance navigators directly.

ch7.h2: A {{raise}} that costs {{cost}} is not a raise.
ch7.p1: At {{wage}}, Carlos crosses a benefits cliff. SNAP drops $312. Childcare subsidy phases out. Medicaid lapses. The raise nets a $400/month {{loss}} — and he can't see it until the first paycheck after the offer.
ch7.p2: We do this math for every offer. Honestly. Before the signature. So the next move is a {{real}}.
ch7.chartAria: Wage cliff chart — real take-home by hourly wage
ch7.ariaLabel: The benefits cliff — a $2 raise that costs $400

cliff.tooltipEdge: the cliff edge — SNAP cuts to $48/mo, childcare voucher lapses
cliff.tooltipDestination: Alcon production tech wage — destination job

ch8.line2: We'll just keep tearing it down,
ch8.ctaMeta: ~3 min · web or text · in English or Spanish
ch8.ariaLabel: Find your path — get your plan

assess.heading: Workforce Assessment
assess.workHistoryPlaceholder: Tell us about your work experience...
assess.resumeDesc: Upload a resume to improve your job matches. This step is optional.
assess.barriersDesc: Choose all that apply. We'll match you with resources and jobs that work around these.
assess.barriersMinOne: Select at least one barrier to continue.
assess.recordDesc: Help us find fair-chance employers and check expungement eligibility. All fields are optional -- skip if you prefer.
assess.benefitsDesc: Tell us about your household and any benefits you receive. This helps us show how a new job might affect your benefits. You can skip this step.
assess.scheduleDesc: Let us know your availability so we can match you with the right shifts and transit options.
assess.industriesDesc: Select industries you're interested in. This helps us find better job matches.
assess.creditDesc: Help us understand your credit situation. This stays private and helps us match you with the right resources.
assess.navDesc: Answer a few questions to get your personalized re-entry plan
assess.privacyBadge: We don't store your identity. No login. No tracking. Your data stays on your device.
assess.errorSubmit: Something went wrong submitting your assessment. Please try again.
assess.errorCredit: Credit check could not be completed. Continuing without credit data.

plan.heading: Your Action Plan
plan.noCliff: No significant benefits cliff detected at any wage level.
plan.noRecovery: Net income does not fully recover within the analyzed wage range.
plan.errorNotFound: Session not found. It may have expired.
plan.errorGeneric: Something went wrong loading your plan. Please try again.
plan.errorFallback: Something went wrong loading your plan.
plan.matchesEmptyDesc: We're still looking for the best matches for your profile. Check back soon or update your assessment.
plan.whatsNextDownloadStrong: Download your Career Center Ready Package
plan.whatsNextDownloadDesc: using the button in Step 1 above.
plan.whatsNextAskCaseManagerDesc: and show them your Career Center Ready Package.

credit.heading: Credit Assessment
credit.encouragement: You're taking a great step by understanding your credit. Knowledge is the first step to improvement.

brand.label: GoWork — workforce infrastructure for any American city

github.aria: View the source code on GitHub

titleSequence.title: The Wall
titleSequence.subtitle: An interactive map of Fort Worth, Texas · April 2026

chapter01.title: Chapter 1 — Continental
chapter01.heroQuestion: What's standing between you and a job?
chapter01.hero: What's standing between you and a job?
chapter01.subhero: You shouldn't have to figure out the wall. We do the math, sequence the path, and hand you the plan.
chapter01.editorial: America from above. One pin in the dark — Fort Worth, Texas.
chapter01.carlosLead: We'll meet a worker named Carlos.
chapter01.pullquote: You shouldn't have to figure out the wall.
chapter01.aria: Chapter 1: Continental — top-down view of America

chapter02.title: Chapter 2 — City Arrival
chapter02.body: Carlos lives here. ZIP 76119. East of downtown — past Sundance Square, past the courthouse.
chapter02.editorial: Carlos lives here. ZIP 76119. East of downtown.
chapter02.detail: Trinity Metro routes thread the city in cyan. Half of them don't reach the warehouses.
chapter02.aria: Chapter 2: Fort Worth at altitude. Trinity Metro routes drawn in cyan.

chapter03.title: Chapter 3 — The Neighborhood
chapter03.body: Carlos is 29. ZIP 76119. Single father of one daughter, four years old. Recently released — sentence complete, three years past. He has $300 in cash, no vehicle, and four years of warehouse and forklift experience. He is ready to work. Four barriers stand between him and a paycheck.
chapter03.carlosIntro: Carlos is 29. A father of one — his daughter is four. Three years ago, a misdemeanor theft charge. Sentence complete. Four years of warehouse work behind him; a forklift cert in his pocket. Now he's home, trying to go back to work.
chapter03.aria: Chapter 3: a neighborhood block in 76119. Carlos is introduced.

chapter04.title: The Wall
chapter04.lede: Most workforce sites pretend barriers don't matter. We do the math to prove they do.
chapter04.aria: Chapter 4: The Wall. Four barriers, named one at a time.

chapter04a.title: Criminal record
chapter04a.pullquote: I came home with $300 and a daughter, and the system told me to go to five offices.
chapter04a.aria: Barrier one: criminal record. Tarrant County District Clerk is 4.8 miles away. Bus 4 plus Bus 6 totals 71 minutes.

chapter04b.title: No transit
chapter04b.detail: He lives in 76119. Bus 4 to downtown is 45 minutes. Plus a transfer. Plus a wait.
chapter04b.pullquote: Forty-five minutes is the schedule. Eighty-seven is the day.
chapter04b.aria: Barrier two: no transit. The downtown commute on Bus 4 with a transfer is 87 minutes.

chapter04c.title: No childcare
chapter04c.detail: His daughter is 4. Childcare is $1,200 a month he doesn't have.
chapter04c.pullquote: I can't take a job that starts before her daycare opens.
chapter04c.aria: Barrier three: no childcare. Market rate is $1,200 a month without a subsidy.

chapter04d.title: Bad credit
chapter04d.detail: 540. Late payments. One collection account. Half the apartments won't take him — and one in three jobs runs a credit check.
chapter04d.pullquote: A score I can't see is closing doors I never knocked on.
chapter04d.aria: Barrier four: bad credit. Roughly one in three job listings runs a credit check Carlos would not pass.

chapter05.title: The Labyrinth
chapter05.editorial: 5 offices. 47 forms. Each one says go to the next one.
chapter05.lede: Without GoWork, this is the path. We named it so we could replace it.
chapter05.pullquote: Every office gave me one form and another address.
chapter05.aria: Chapter 5: the Labyrinth. Five offices and forty-seven forms — the chaos before GoWork sequences the path.
chapter05.convergenceCaption: 47 forms. 5 offices. We sequence them. You walk once.

barrierLabels.credit: Bad credit → Legal Aid NW Texas

chapter06.title: The Math
chapter06.hero: When more pay means less money.
chapter06.subhero: We do the cliff math so the choice is honest. Move the slider — watch the cliff.
chapter06.body: Carlos stocks shelves at Amazon FC DFW5 — 71 minutes from home on Bus 4. A $2/hour raise sounds like a win. The benefits cliff says otherwise: childcare subsidy gone at $14/hr, SNAP gone at $17/hr. The chart is the receipt.
chapter06.statLabel: from Carlos's home to DFW5 via Bus 4
chapter06.wageSliderHint: Drag to see the cliff move with the wage.
chapter06.cliffChartLabel: Net monthly income vs. hourly wage
chapter06.cliffChartCaption: Demo cliff projection — Carlos's profile (single father, 1 dependent, 76119, no child support).
chapter06.aria: Chapter 6: The Math. Camera lands on Amazon Fulfillment Center DFW5. A wage slider drives the benefits cliff chart in real time so you can see how a raise turns into a loss.

chapter07.title: Your case file. Today.
chapter07.hero: Don't walk for twelve weeks. Walk in once. With everything.
chapter07.subhero: GoWork hands you a case file the same day. You take it to a case worker. You pull jobs. You apply. You go to work.
chapter07.body: We assemble your barriers, your eligibility, your transit options, your job matches into one PDF. Carlos doesn't bounce between five offices — he rides Trinity Metro Bus 4 and Bus 6 once, hands the case file to Workforce Solutions, and starts work that week.
chapter07.aria: Chapter 7: Your case file. Today. Carlos's same-day plan walks one stop with everything in hand.

chapter08.title: Chapter 8 — The Graph
chapter08.hero: The wall isn't a list. It's a graph.
chapter08.subhero: Every barrier connects to two more. Resolve one and three others move within reach. We mapped them so we could route around them.
chapter08.body: Thirty-three nodes. Seven categories. The constellation breathes above the city — every edge a dependency, every node a barrier we know how to name.
chapter08.aria: Chapter 8: The Graph. A three-dimensional barrier constellation hovers above downtown Fort Worth.
chapter08.fallbackAlt: Static rendering of the 33-node barrier constellation

chapter09.title: Any City
chapter09.hero: It works in Fort Worth. It will work where you are.
chapter09.subhero: One city lit. Five dotted in. The math, the path, the plan — wherever the work is.
chapter09.body: GoWork ships as a city template. Fort Worth is the reference deployment. Five more Texas cities are on deck — same engine, same playbook, local data.
chapter09.statValue: Built in Fort Worth. Free to use.
chapter09.statLabel: Wherever the work is — we go where you go.
chapter09.aria: Chapter 9: Any City. The camera returns to a Texas-wide view. Fort Worth glows as the reference deployment; five Texas cities — Dallas, Houston, Austin, San Antonio, and Waco — are dotted on deck. A button tours the Texas region; another returns to Fort Worth.

chapter10.title: Find your path
chapter10.subhero: We do the math. We sequence the route. You take the first step.
chapter10.body: You've seen the wall. You've seen the labyrinth. Now skip both — start your assessment and we'll hand you a case file the same day. You take it to a case worker. You apply. You go to work.
chapter10.aria: Chapter 10: Find your path. Begin your assessment to receive a personalized plan.

404.title: There is no path to this URL
404.body: But there is one through the wall. Every barrier connects — let's get you back on the route.

500.title: Something stalled
500.body: We're calibrating. The page hit a snag, our team has been notified, and the request was logged. Try again, or step back to the home view.

empty.title: Nothing to chart yet
empty.body: Once data lands here, the path will draw itself.

privacy.heading: Privacy Policy
privacy.counselNotice: Hackathon-grade placeholder — this policy was authored fresh for the GoWork submission and has not been reviewed by counsel. Production rollout requires legal review.
privacy.esPlaceholder: (Spanish-only placeholder string; never rendered when locale is English.)

collect.heading: What we collect
collect.intro: We collect only what we need to run the service. Specifically:

processors.heading: Third-party processors
processors.intro: We use a small set of vendors to deliver the service. They process data on our behalf (they are processors, not controllers) and are contractually bound to use it only for the purpose described.

rights.heading: Your rights
rights.intro: You have the right to see, correct, and delete the data we hold about you, plus the right to know what we have. Each right has a one-click action in the app:

terms.heading: Terms of Service
terms.counselNotice: Hackathon-grade placeholder — these terms were authored fresh for the GoWork submission and have not been reviewed by counsel. Production rollout requires legal review.
terms.esPlaceholder: (Spanish-only placeholder string; never rendered when locale is English.)

404.title: Page not found
404.body: The page you were looking for doesn't exist or has moved. Let's get you back on track.

500.title: Something went wrong
500.body: We hit an unexpected snag. The request was logged and our team will take a look. You can try again or head back home.
